g.title: Wooden Arm Piece with Holes (x2)
g.desc: This is an elongated, 5mm thick plywood piece with a gentle curve along its length. It has two rounded ends, with one end being noticeably wider than the other. To identify it, place the piece flat. Locate the wider, rounded end. Along the centerline, starting from this wider end, you will first feel a small circular hole. Moving towards the narrower end, the next feature is a teardrop-shaped hole; the wider, rounded part of the teardrop points towards the wider end of the piece. After that, you'll find a rectangular slot, and finally, at the very end of the narrower section, there's another small circular hole. You will need two identical 'Arm' pieces.

g.title: Completed Ramp and Track Assembly Overview
g.desc: This diagram shows the finished STEM kit. A long orange track with a U-shaped channel is mounted on a laser-cut wooden frame. The track starts at a high angle, held up by two large, vertical wooden supports, and curves down to a flat exit. At the bottom section, two black rectangular side panels are bolted to the wooden frame on either side of the track. All components are fastened together using small brass screws and nuts through pre-drilled holes in the plywood and track.

g.title: Large Base Piece with Four Holes
g.desc: Identify this single tall wooden part by its wide rectangular section at the bottom. The narrow vertical portion above the base contains four small rectangular holes aligned vertically in the center. Near the bottom base, there are two T-shaped notches on each side edge for interlocking with other pieces.

g.title: Identify Two Plastic Track Pieces
g.desc: Find the two plastic track pieces in your kit. Unlike the rigid wooden parts, these are made of smooth, slightly flexible plastic. Each piece is a long, flat strip with two raised edges running along its length, forming a channel or groove. Feel the ends of each track piece for connecting tabs or slots that allow them to link together.

g.title: Locate the Large Slope Wood Piece
g.desc: This is one flat piece of 5mm thick plywood. It has a symmetrical, elongated shape, similar to a dog bone or a double-ended wrench. Feel the straight, narrower middle section, and then notice that both ends are identical, each flaring out and curving into two rounded projections. The entire piece has smooth, laser-cut edges.

g.title: Small Base Wood Component
g.desc: Find one flat, rectangular piece of 5mm thick laser-cut plywood. This is the 'Small Base'. Hold the piece so that the two short ends are on your left and right, and the two long ends are at the top and bottom. On the right short end, you will feel two distinct 'T'-shaped tabs sticking out, one near the top corner and one near the bottom corner. On the left short end, you'll find two simpler rectangular tabs sticking out, also one near the top and one near the bottom. Both the long top edge and the long bottom edge each have a rectangular notch cut out in their centers. In the main flat area of the piece, centered horizontally, you will feel two small circular holes, one towards the left and one towards the right, for #4-40 brass hardware.

g.title: One Rectangular Track Spacer Piece
g.desc: This is the Track Spacer piece. It is a flat, approximately 5-millimeter thick component, which could be smooth acrylic or slightly textured plywood. Tactilely, feel its two long, straight edges and two shorter, rounded ends, making it a 'pill' or 'capsule' shape. You will find two round holes, one near each rounded end, centered along the piece's length. These holes are for the #4-40 brass hardware and help you orient the piece by matching them to corresponding holes in other components.

g.title: Identify the Track Connector
g.desc: Find the thin, flat track connector made of FR4 circuit board material, which is about 1 millimeter thick. This piece feels like a small, smooth rectangle with all four corners rounded. Hold it so its longer sides are horizontal. You will feel two identical small circular holes, one near each rounded end, placed along the center line of the piece.

g.title: Small Slope Plywood Component
g.desc: This is one piece of 5mm thick laser-cut plywood. Feel for its symmetrical shape, similar to a dog bone or a double-ended wrench. It has a narrower central section that widens at both ends. Each end features two smooth, rounded indentations that curve inward, making it feel like a pair of gentle hooks facing each other. This distinct shape helps you identify the single 'Small Slope' piece from your kit.

g.title: Wooden Protractor Component Pair
g.desc: This is one of two identical 5mm thick laser-cut plywood pieces. Feel its distinct irregular shape, which has a flat bottom edge. On the left end, you will find a rectangular cutout. Moving right, there are two circular through-holes. The right side of the piece curves upward like a protractor, where you can tactually feel a series of engraved lines and numbers (like 0, 20, 40 degrees). The longer engraved lines mark the specific angles. Use the rectangular cutout on one end and the curved protractor scale on the other to help orient the piece.

g.title: Main Side Panel (Two Pieces)
g.desc: Find a large, flat, 5mm thick wood panel that is mostly rectangular with rounded corners. Run your fingers along the edges to locate a unique, rounded, hook-like extension on the bottom right corner. This helps you orient the piece. Hold the panel so this 'hook' is at your bottom right. Along the top edge, you will feel two small, vertical rectangular slots, one near the left and one near the right. In the center of the top half, there is a larger, horizontal rectangular slot. Around this top area, you'll also find four small circular holes. Below the large central slot, you can feel the engraved letters 'PIPER' and a website address. To the far left, you might feel a small, circular engraving with the letter 'B' inside. Towards the bottom of the panel, there are two more horizontal rectangular slots, one on the left and one on the right, and a final small circular hole near the bottom right, just before the hook-like extension. There are two identical pieces like this in your kit.

g.title: Gummy Feet (x4)
g.desc: Locate four small pieces that feel soft, squishy, and flexible, unlike the hard plywood or metal parts. These are your gummy feet. Each foot has one flat side, which may feel a little sticky or have a smooth, thin paper covering you can peel off. The other side should feel rounded or dome-shaped. The flat, sticky side is the one that will attach to the bottom of your plywood base. Remember, once these feet are stuck on, they are very difficult to remove.

g.title: Two IR Beam Sensors
g.desc: Locate two identical small electronic components made of plastic. Each sensor feels like a flat, small rectangle, typically about an inch long and half an inch wide. On one of the shorter ends, you will feel a smooth, rounded lens or dome that resembles a small button. From the opposite end, stiff metal pins will extend. These are your IR beam sensors.

g.title: Main Ramp Component, One Piece
g.desc: Locate the single, flat wooden ramp piece, about 5mm thick. It is longer than it is wide. Feel one end for two distinct 'T'-shaped tabs that stick out from the top corners. This is the top of the ramp. The opposite end, the bottom, has wider but shallower cutouts at each corner. Along both long sides, roughly in the middle, you will also feel an indentation. Run your fingers down the center of the ramp to find four evenly spaced rectangular holes.

g.title: Component A: 1/4 inch Pan-Head Screws
g.desc: This image shows four small brass pan-head screws, identified as Component A. To find them tactilely, feel for a screw with a rounded, dome-shaped top, like an upside-down pan, which is flat underneath. These #4-40 screws are 1/4 inch long; measure from the underside of the head to the tip of the screw. The shaft is fully threaded. The brass hardware typically feels smooth and sturdy compared to other materials. Locate four identical screws matching this description.

g.title: Component B: Flat-head Screws (x2)
g.desc: Find two small, metallic brass screws. Each screw is 1/2 inch long, which is about half the length of your thumb from the first joint to the tip. Feel the top of the screw head; it should be completely flat, and then it tapers sharply downwards like a cone. On the flat top, you should feel a cross-shaped indentation for a screwdriver. The rest of the screw is a thin shaft covered in spiraling ridges, called threads. These are your 'B' flat-head screws.

g.title: Locate 1/2-inch Pan-head Screws
g.desc: Locate 8 brass pan-head screws, each 1/2 inch long. Feel for a small, smooth metal piece. One end has a rounded, slightly domed top, like an upside-down pan, with a cross-shaped indentation for a screwdriver. This is the screw head. The other end is a thin shaft with spiraling, rough threads. Measure the length from the underside of the head to the tip to ensure it's 1/2 inch. This is component 'C'.

g.title: Eight #4-40 Brass Hex Nuts
g.desc: Locate eight small brass nuts. Each nut is a tiny cylinder, about the size of a fingernail. Feel around the outer edge for six flat sides, forming a hexagonal shape. In the center, there is a round hole with ridges, or threads, on the inside, which is where a screw will go.
